rationale: Keep this pair because the citing paper explicitly studies flat-rate/pay-per-use tariff-choice biases and tests usage misestimation mechanisms, while the cited paper develops a cognitive model of usage estimation and flat-rate bias (including break-even likelihood and the ratio rule). Given this strong construct/mechanism overlap and the citing paper’s stated extension of prior work on these biases, the citation is plausibly foundational or theory-supplying rather than an incidental mention.
